specific_location: Lagerman Reservoir and the Swede Lakes
description: A smallish shorebird that was a little larger than the many Baird's Sandpipers that were present. A buffy head and upper chest with a dark eye and a prominent light tan complete eyering. Short, thin dark bill and longish yellow legs. The belly was white. The lower neck and the back had a scaly pattern with the feathers fringed in white, which is why I think it was a juvenile bird.  The bird often foraged well away from the water's edge on the drier parts of the shore which had quite a lot of vegetation.
photographer: Photographer: Kevin Rutherford., Christian Nunes., Christian Nunes.
